data.epi_content:
I felt a strong recoil in my arm, and something exploded through the air, giving me the sensation of metal fragments cutting through the space ahead.

This indicated only one thing.

The bullet had been fired.

Was that shadow truly human, or was it a flawless machine?

I believe it's the former.

If it had been magic or a spell that activated conditionally, the Crown Prince wouldn't have been hit by what I shot.

Yes, the Crown Prince was definitely hit by the bullet I fired.

After the bullet was fired, I clearly heard a faint scream.

If that weren't the case, I might feel somewhat desperate.

If after tearing a hole in my own throat, somehow rising through sheer malice to turn the gun and fire, only to be blocked again—that would mean there's no way for me to deal with the Crown Prince directly in this world.

So rather than merely hoping he was hit, I'll firmly believe he was.

"Grrrgh..."

Even though I haven't confirmed the truth yet, just thinking about it makes emotions well up inside me.

I tried to say something with a sense of elation, but all that came out was the sound of blood bubbling.

Blood must be gushing madly from my throat.

Every slight movement made my clothes feel wet and sloshing with liquid.

Sure enough, as I looked down, I saw dizziness and drowsiness washing over me, along with countless bloodstains.

My clothes were so thoroughly stained red that if someone claimed they were originally white, they'd be told to stop joking.

I must have damaged something vital.

My vision is blurry.

But somehow, I desperately wanted to confirm what had happened to Johannes, that cursed individual who deserved damnation.

So even though my body was spurting blood everywhere, I somehow summoned my dying strength and forced myself to stand.

If you asked if it hurt, I'd say it hurt like hell.

But I needed someone to curse.

Someone I could hate with all my life, resent, and want to destroy.

I couldn't curse or hate him no matter how much I wanted to.

Whether it was ingrained in my blood, or whether the time spent with Erica made it impossible to hate him, whenever I looked at Evan, even if I disliked him, I never once thought about wanting to kill him.

I could only harbor resentment about why Evan had abandoned me, why Evan hadn't cared about me.

...As for Vivian, I was afraid to even look up at her.

If the Crown Prince tried to kill Vivian, a meteor would probably fall on the Crown Prince's head, killing him in some absurd way.

That's the kind of world this is.

No matter how great the Empire claims to be, if it tried to kill Vivian the way it's trying to kill me now,

Vivian would either directly designate the Empire as evil and defeat it, or the Empire would self-destruct.

In the end, it's the Crown Prince.

If only the Crown Prince were gone, I could not only keep my life intact but also flee far away from Vivian.

People need goals to have a driving force in life.

It gives them a reason to live.

One can't deny that even negative emotions can help with that.

I cleared my thoughts from my dizzy mind and raised my body.

When I stretched out my arm to stand up, my strength suddenly gave out and I staggered, but I gathered my strength again and rose.

The scene I saw upon standing was the Crown Prince clutching his right eye, blood streaming down.

It was quite a spectacle.
data.epi_content2:
"Argh, huff, this is maddening. It's been so long since I felt pain..."

As I staggered forward, the Crown Prince was clutching his right eye and stumbling, bracing himself against the floor.

"Your head...grgh..."

I wanted to say, "Let me put a bullet in your head right now," but because of the bubbling blood rising in my throat, I just shook my head once to clear my mind and walked toward the Crown Prince.

Thinking I'd gotten a bit closer, I aimed the gun at the Crown Prince, who was staggering and clutching his right eye, with my trembling left hand that hadn't yet fallen off.

A smile formed on my lips without me realizing it.

Like a crawling insect, he held his right eye with one hand and wriggled backward, trying to escape from me.

But the fact that I couldn't keep up with his pace by walking made this truly a chase between cripples.

I sighed once and aimed as accurately as I could, then pulled the trigger.

"Urgh!"

Once again, with the violent explosive sound characteristic of firearms, the bullet hit the Crown Prince.

His whole body was writhing.

My vision isn't clear.

It's just blurry and hazy, with only vague shapes visible.

Feeling like I might collapse and die as time passed, I quickly pulled the trigger and fired all the remaining bullets.

I need to reload, but I realized I didn't bring any extra bullets.

Sitting down, I cursed my now immobile body and tried to get up again by wriggling somehow.

Not that it helped me stand.

And so, the Crown Prince, who was still writhing in front of me, and I remained there, unable to move.

How much time passed like that? Having lost so much blood, my consciousness was gradually fading, and I started thinking I would die soon when the figure before my eyes began to move.

Then I heard a despairing voice.

"...Ugh, to think they treated my body first instead of immediately dealing with the dangerous individual.

If my head had been blown off, what were these fools thinking, making such arbitrary decisions?"

As he spoke, he began to poke at something on the floor.

Suddenly, the floor became soaked with black liquid.

I didn't particularly want to know what it was, and it was just unpleasant.

"Haha, ha. Haaa."

Seeing the Crown Prince speaking normally and moving around, I wanted to scream and wail, but my body, drained of blood, couldn't even muster the strength for such a reaction.

"It's fortunate the sound-blocking magic is still intact.

Even my sister couldn't touch the head area before.

You almost succeeded in a historic assassination, isn't that a shame?"

Rather than feeling regret, I was actually moved by the thought that I had hit him with bullets.

I thought he would be completely unharmed, not even a single bullet lodged in him.

By the way, if one blows off his head, would he truly be unable to do anything?

"To blow your own head off and immediately think to turn the gun..."

He keeps rambling on, calling me insane, saying that even if you brought a veteran from the battlefield, few people could do such a thing.
data.epi_content3:
I was getting tired of his voice.

As the sound began to buzz and echo, my head started to throb.

The Crown Prince right in front of me wasn't even visible, yet Evan standing far away was so clearly visible.

His hand, bleeding from clenching his fist too tightly, and his wrist—not held by anyone but dark...no, is that a tattoo?

It looked as if he were wearing handcuffs, firmly restrained.

He was biting his lip so hard that blood was trickling down his chin, and tears of blood from his eyes followed that trail, forming droplets at the bottom that fell one by one.

With his mouth, he seemed to be repeating that he would save me.

If he's going to be in such pain and anger, I wish he would save me right now.

What can he save when he can't even move?

If he saved me, he'd be nothing short of a prince on a white horse.

If he saved me, I'd give him a kiss, if not as a princess, then as a noble lady.

Save me.

As I looked at Evan, ignoring the slightly tender feelings rising within me, the Crown Prince let out a sinister laugh and grabbed my chin, looking around.

I felt the hole in my neck gradually closing.

"It would be too wasteful to throw you to my lustful cousin."

My blurry vision began to clear gradually.

Along with the slightly dulled pain and the throbbing in my head.

And as my head slowly returned to normal, it became dizzyingly chaotic again.

The Crown Prince, with a furious, scowling face, clenched his fist and began to beat me mercilessly.

Even as I was hit repeatedly, my body was being healed perfectly while only the pain remained.

The agony and disconnect made me feel like my eyes would roll back.

After beating me for a long time, he seemed to tire, rotated his wrist once, and then delivered a punch to my face with all his might.

I collapsed to the floor and could only shed tears, wondering when I would finally die.

"Yes, they say a beating is medicine for a madman.

Now that you've been hit, you know how to cry. That's good.

If I were to lose the Crown Prince position, becoming a doctor managing the mentally ill in the countryside wouldn't be so bad."

I'm starting to get tired of hearing this nonsense.

"...Sigh, I'll have to keep you alive and take you with me.

It would be best to say that you went mad after learning your family had collapsed while you were at the Academy, and then disappeared somewhere while wandering outside."

With those final words, he gritted his teeth.

So petty about getting hit by a few bullets.

At his words, the shadow at his feet, soaked in black liquid, grabbed me and began dragging me to where he was.

Resistance was futile.

Unable to escape, I was dragged away into an unknown space beneath the Crown Prince.

A place filled with the smell of blood and the distinctive scent of rusted iron.

Below were nameless objects, bottles filled with potions, and several beds.

The most noticeable was a creepy-looking bed covered in red and black blood.

A floating cloud lifted me and placed me there.

That was my last memory of the day I was dragged to the Imperial Palace.
